slots: Ziggurat motifs, nautical reliefs, Queen’s Necklace

Begin at Girgaon Chowpatty and walk south along the Backbay — identify Streamline Moderne balconies, porthole windows, and pastel façades that earned Mumbai the world’s second-largest Art Deco inventory.
Victorian public buildings, cricket greens, UNESCO ensemble
Stand on the maidan where the 19th-century Gothic university and high court face the 1930s Art Deco row — the core of the 2018 UNESCO inscription.
Experience the living city that inhabits these heritage streets — the ensemble is valued as an urban cultural landscape, not frozen architecture.
Study the streamlined cinema halls and shop-house Deco in Colaba — many façades survive above street-level signage.
Partner UNESCO site, Victorian Gothic anchor
Cross to the Fort side of the maidan to see how Gothic and Deco were deliberately juxtaposed in British Bombay’s civic planning.
An evening in the arts quarter where conservation groups run heritage walks and document Deco details.
View the Art Deco skyline from the harbour — the sea approach reveals how the Backbay ensemble steps down toward the water.
Explore lesser-known Deco neighbourhoods north of the peninsula with a local architecture guide.
Close with the modern city’s relationship to its coast — a reminder that UNESCO protection here celebrates a still-evolving metropolis.
